data.text: I'll start by reading the manifest, plan, and relevant source files before making any changes.

input.description: Read governing files and explore Gemini codebase
input.prompt:

1. .ddx/beads-harness-interface-refactor.yaml
2. docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-14-harness-interface-refactor.md

- GeminiQuotaSnapshot
- GeminiQuotaRoutingDecision
- AuthSnapshot
- ReadAuthEvidence
- GeminiAuthFreshnessWindow
- DefaultGeminiModelDiscovery
- ResolveGeminiModelAlias
- RefreshAsync (in gemini-related files)

List all relevant file paths found. Also look at the directory structure of the gemini harness package. Report thoroughly so I can understand the full scope of changes needed.

7	**Rough size**: 12 numbered steps totaling ~20–25 PRs over 6–10 weeks for one engineer serialized (less with parallelism across the per-harness migrations after Step 4 lands); Steps 5/6/7 each land as 4–6 sub-PRs
8	
9	## Problem Statement
10	
11	The service depends on roughly 80 exported symbols across the per-harness
12	packages beyond the documented `Harness` interface — quota cache I/O,
13	routing-decision functions, concrete snapshot types with field-level
14	reads, family-alias resolvers, PTY probe entry points, and account-file
15	readers. A 2026-05-14 inventory counted **69 external call sites** across
16	six service files. The `Harness` interface alone does not capture the
17	contract; adding any new harness today requires either duplicating ~25
18	symbols under a new prefix or rewiring service code per-harness.
19	
20	CONTRACT-004 defines the universal interface. This plan ports every
21	existing harness, every service consumer, every test, and the
22	public-facing CONTRACT-003 projection onto that contract, then enforces
23	the boundary with a lint rule.
24	
25	## Requirements
26	
27	### Functional
28	
29	- Every existing harness implements the CONTRACT-004 interfaces it
30	  qualifies for: `Harness` (all), `QuotaHarness` (claude, codex,
31	  gemini), `AccountHarness` (gemini; optional for claude/codex),
32	  `ModelDiscoveryHarness` (all five).
33	- Service-side code reads quota, routing preference, account state,
34	  and model discovery exclusively through the interfaces. No file in
35	  `service*.go`, `internal/serviceimpl/`, `internal/runtimesignals/`,
36	  or `cmd/` imports per-harness package symbols beyond the runner
37	  constructor used by the dispatcher.
38	- Per-harness `*QuotaSnapshot`, `*QuotaRoutingDecision`,
39	  `*QuotaCachePath`, `Read*Quota*`, `Write*Quota*`,
40	  `Default*QuotaStaleAfter`, `Decide*QuotaRouting`,
41	  `Default*ModelDiscovery`, and `Resolve*Alias` symbols are removed
42	  from the public surface (deleted or unexported).
43	- Public CONTRACT-003 JSON shapes (`HarnessInfo`, `ProviderInfo`,
44	  `QuotaState`, `AccountStatus`) remain structurally identical (same
45	  field set, same value semantics) to pre-refactor recorded fixtures.
46	- The service has a single async refresh scheduler that consumes
47	  `QuotaHarness.QuotaFreshness()` and `AccountHarness.AccountFreshness()`
48	  to drive refreshes, replacing per-harness `Refresh*Async` functions.
49	
50	### Non-Functional
51	
52	- Refactor is staged one harness at a time so regressions surface
53	  early.
54	- The lint rule that enforces the boundary lands in the last step;
55	  partial migrations cannot accidentally satisfy it.
56	- No record-mode cassette is invalidated; cache file on-disk JSON
57	  shapes remain stable (cache file schemas use field-tagged JSON,
58	  not type-name encoding).
59	- Pre-existing test suite runtime does not regress by more than 10%
60	  (measured as `go test -count=1 ./... -timeout 30m` wall time before
61	  Step 0 and after the final step, excluding new test packages
62	  introduced by Steps 2 and 4). No individual pre-existing test slows
63	  by more than 10%. New conformance and scheduler tests are budgeted
64	  and reviewed separately in the Step 11 PR.

570	## Rollback Strategy
571	
572	Each step's PR is independently revertable.
573	
574	- **Cache file compatibility**: All per-harness cache files use
575	  field-tagged JSON encoding. Renaming the Go type from
576	  `ClaudeQuotaSnapshot` to `claudeQuotaSnapshot` does not change the
577	  on-disk JSON shape (verified for each harness in Step 5a/6a/7a
578	  before unexporting). A revert of Step 5e reintroduces the exported
579	  type name without invalidating cached files written under the new
580	  code.
581	- **Per-harness rollback**: Reverting Step 5 (claude) does not require
582	  reverting Steps 6–8 (codex/gemini/opencode/pi) because each
583	  harness's interface implementation is independent. The reverted
584	  harness goes back to its per-harness public surface; the others
585	  remain on the interface.
586	- **Pre-lint coexistence**: Step 10's lint rule lands last
587	  intentionally so that partial-state environments (some harnesses
588	  migrated, some not) compile and test. A revert during this window
589	  produces a working tree; a revert after Step 10 may require
590	  reverting Step 10 alongside.
591	- **Public JSON shape**: Step 11's structural-diff assertion is the
592	  guarantee that any merged state preserves CONTRACT-003. A diff at
593	  Step 11 blocks merge; rollback is a revert of the offending step,
594	  not a forward-fix on a broken contract.
595	
596	## Risks and Mitigations
597	
598	| Risk | Mitigation |
599	|------|------------|
600	| JSON shape regression during projection refactor | Step 0 fixtures pinned before any refactor work; Step 11 asserts structural equality with documented time-field handling. |
601	| Routing semantics drift (boolean `PreferX` → `RoutingPreference` enum) | Step 5 (claude) lands fully and runs the routing test suite before Step 6 (codex) begins. Each subsequent harness re-runs the suite. |
602	| Cache file schema drift when snapshots are renamed | Cache files use field-tagged JSON; type renames don't affect on-disk shape. Each Step 5a/6a/7a includes a load-old-write-new round-trip test before unexporting. |
603	| Conformance suite is too strict and blocks legitimate variation | Conformance covers only CONTRACT-004 contract behavior. Harness-specific tests stay in the harness package. The suite is itself tested in Step 2 against the synthetic harness. |
604	| Lint exemption file accumulates as a backdoor | The exemption list in Step 10 is one line; growing it requires an ADR amendment. |
605	| Refactor stalls partway, leaving mixed surface | Step 10 (lint rule) lands last; partial state is a valid intermediate. Each harness migration's sub-sequence (5e/6e/7e/8) deletes the old surface as an explicit acceptance gate before the next harness starts. |
606	| `claude-tui` work resurfaces during the refactor and bypasses it | ADR-013 is withdrawn (not paused); re-proposal must cite the merged CONTRACT-004. |
607	| Async refresh scheduler is wrong and refreshes too often or too rarely | Step 4's scheduler tests use a fake clock and assert exact tick counts per `QuotaFreshness()` value. Production scheduling defects are caught by `service_status` integration tests measuring actual refresh cadence. |
608	| Step 5 sub-sequence stalls partway (5b lands, 5c does not) | Intermediate states (5a-5d) are valid: claude's new interface methods coexist with the old surface. The old surface only disappears in 5e. |

16	// One debounced ticker is scheduled per registered QuotaHarness at

18	// registered AccountHarness only when its AccountFreshness() differs
19	// from the harness's QuotaFreshness() — when the cadences match, the
20	// QuotaHarness ticker is sufficient for callers that want to drive
21	// account refreshes alongside quota.
22	//
23	// The scheduler does not enforce single-flight itself. RefreshQuota /
24	// RefreshAccount are required by CONTRACT-004 to be single-flight per
25	// harness instance (via the harness's cache lock); the scheduler relies
26	// on that contract. Cache-freshness deduplication, however, is the
27	// scheduler's responsibility: it reads QuotaStatus before each refresh
28	// and skips ticks where CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() of now,
29	// so that RefreshQuota itself can probe unconditionally per
30	// CONTRACT-004 invariant #4.

34	// AccountHarness. Type assertions fail and the scheduler registers

55	// The production implementation wraps time.Now / time.NewTicker; tests
56	// inject a controllable fake clock to drive cadence deterministically.
57	type schedulerClock interface {
58		Now() time.Time
59		NewTicker(d time.Duration) schedulerTicker
60	}

100	// type-asserts to QuotaHarness and AccountHarness independently. For
101	// each capability the harness satisfies, it:
102	//
103	//  1. Reads the current cached status. When the status reports
104	//     QuotaUnavailable (cache-cold), the scheduler fires one
105	//     immediate refresh.
106	//  2. Starts a debounced ticker at Freshness/2 cadence that drives
107	//     subsequent refreshes.
108	//
109	// Start is not safe to call concurrently; concurrent Start panics.
110	// Call Stop before re-starting.
111	func (s *refreshScheduler) Start(parent context.Context) {
112		s.mu.Lock()
113		if s.cancel != nil {
114			s.mu.Unlock()
115			panic("refreshScheduler: Start called twice without intervening Stop")
116		}
117		s.ctx, s.cancel = context.WithCancel(parent)
118		s.mu.Unlock()
119	
120		now := s.clock.Now()
121		for _, name := range s.names {
122			h := s.lookup(name)
123			if h == nil {
124				continue
125			}
126			qh, isQuota := h.(harnesses.QuotaHarness)
127			ah, isAccount := h.(harnesses.AccountHarness)
128	
129			if isQuota {
130				s.startQuotaTicker(name, qh, now)
131			}
132			if isAccount {
133				// Only start a separate account ticker when account
134				// freshness differs from quota freshness. When they
135				// match, the quota cadence already covers the cache
136				// lifetime account refresh would observe; harnesses
137				// that want account-aligned refresh can implement it
138				// inside RefreshQuota. See CONTRACT-004 / Step 4.
139				if !isQuota || ah.AccountFreshness() != qh.QuotaFreshness() {
140					s.startAccountTicker(name, ah, now)
141				}
142			}
143		}
144	}
145	
146	// Stop cancels the scheduler context and waits for every ticker
147	// goroutine to exit. Safe to call on a never-started scheduler (no-op).
148	func (s *refreshScheduler) Stop() {
149		s.mu.Lock()
150		cancel := s.cancel
151		s.cancel = nil
152		s.mu.Unlock()
153		if cancel == nil {
154			return
155		}
156		cancel()
157		s.wg.Wait()
158	}
159	
160	// startQuotaTicker fires the cache-cold kickoff (if applicable) and
161	// launches the per-harness debounced ticker.
162	func (s *refreshScheduler) startQuotaTicker(name string, qh harnesses.QuotaHarness, now time.Time) {
163		interval := qh.QuotaFreshness() / 2
164		if interval <= 0 {
165			// Misconfigured harness: a non-positive freshness disables
166			// scheduling — the scheduler refuses to busy-loop. Surfaced
167			// as a CONTRACT-004 violation by the conformance suite.
168			return
169		}
170	
171		// Cache-cold startup kickoff. Per
172		// primary-harness-capability-baseline.md and AC #6, the
173		// scheduler fires one RefreshQuota immediately when the
174		// cached state is QuotaUnavailable.
175		if status, err := qh.QuotaStatus(s.ctx, now); err == nil && status.State == harnesses.QuotaUnavailable {
176			s.wg.Add(1)
177			go func() {
178				defer s.wg.Done()
179				_, _ = qh.RefreshQuota(s.ctx)
180			}()
181		}
182	
183		ticker := s.clock.NewTicker(interval)
184		s.wg.Add(1)
185		go func() {
186			defer s.wg.Done()
187			defer ticker.Stop()
188			for {
189				select {
190				case <-s.ctx.Done():
191					return
192				case <-ticker.C():
193					s.maybeRefreshQuota(name, qh)
194				}
195			}
196		}()
197	}
198	
199	// maybeRefreshQuota implements the per-tick decision. On cache-cold
200	// (QuotaUnavailable) it always refreshes. Otherwise it skips when
201	// CapturedAt is within QuotaFreshness() of now (cache-freshness dedup
202	// is the scheduler's job, not RefreshQuota's). All other tick
203	// outcomes result in a RefreshQuota call.
204	func (s *refreshScheduler) maybeRefreshQuota(name string, qh harnesses.QuotaHarness) {
205		defer s.notifyTick(name)
206	
207		now := s.clock.Now()
208		status, err := qh.QuotaStatus(s.ctx, now)
209		if err != nil {
210			return
211		}
212	
213		if status.State == harnesses.QuotaUnavailable {
214			_, _ = qh.RefreshQuota(s.ctx)
215			return
216		}
217	
218		if !status.CapturedAt.IsZero() && now.Sub(status.CapturedAt) < qh.QuotaFreshness() {
219			return
220		}
221	
222		_, _ = qh.RefreshQuota(s.ctx)
223	}
224	
225	// startAccountTicker is the AccountHarness analogue of
226	// startQuotaTicker. Cache-cold is detected via a zero CapturedAt
227	// because AccountSnapshot has no explicit "unavailable" state value.
228	func (s *refreshScheduler) startAccountTicker(name string, ah harnesses.AccountHarness, now time.Time) {
229		interval := ah.AccountFreshness() / 2
230		if interval <= 0 {
231			return
232		}
233	
234		if snap, err := ah.AccountStatus(s.ctx, now); err == nil && snap.CapturedAt.IsZero() {
235			s.wg.Add(1)
236			go func() {
237				defer s.wg.Done()
238				_, _ = ah.RefreshAccount(s.ctx)
239			}()
240		}
241	
242		ticker := s.clock.NewTicker(interval)
243		s.wg.Add(1)
244		go func() {
245			defer s.wg.Done()
246			defer ticker.Stop()
247			for {
248				select {
249				case <-s.ctx.Done():
250					return
251				case <-ticker.C():
252					s.maybeRefreshAccount(name, ah)
253				}
254			}
255		}()
256	}
257	
258	// maybeRefreshAccount mirrors maybeRefreshQuota for AccountHarness.
259	func (s *refreshScheduler) maybeRefreshAccount(name string, ah harnesses.AccountHarness) {
260		defer s.notifyTick(name)
261	
262		now := s.clock.Now()
263		snap, err := ah.AccountStatus(s.ctx, now)
264		if err != nil {
265			return
266		}
267	
268		if snap.CapturedAt.IsZero() {
269			_, _ = ah.RefreshAccount(s.ctx)
270			return
271		}
272	
273		if now.Sub(snap.CapturedAt) < ah.AccountFreshness() {
274			return
275		}
276	
277		_, _ = ah.RefreshAccount(s.ctx)
278	}
279	
280	// notifyTick fans out a tick-processed notification when tickNotify is
281	// set. Never blocks the scheduler goroutine on the receiver.
282	func (s *refreshScheduler) notifyTick(name string) {
283		if s.tickNotify == nil {
284			return
285		}
286		select {
287		case s.tickNotify <- name:
288		case <-s.ctx.Done():
289		}
290	}
